mnemonics: Many Monkeys Jump On Jane's Lap
Mad Men Jump On Jubilant Nights
keyPoints: Arrays are a type of data structure in programming that can store multiple values in a single variable.
One-dimensional arrays can store values in a single row, while two-dimensional arrays can store values in multiple rows and columns.
Initializing a two-dimensional array requires specifying the number of rows and columns.
The number of elements in a two-dimensional array is calculated by multiplying the number of rows by the number of columns.
Arrays can be used to store and manipulate large amounts of data efficiently.
Arrays are a fundamental data structure in programming and are used in many applications.
Initializing a two-dimensional array can be done using the following syntax: array_name = [[element1, element2, ..., elementN], [element1, element2, ..., elementN], ...];
Arrays can be accessed using their indices, which are zero-based and start from the top-left corner of the array.
